image_prompts.prompt: Hook visual: Science has a clearer story about pulsars than social media does.
Context visual: Pulsars are highly magnetized, rotating neutron stars, emitting beams of radiation.
Escalation visual: They spin rapidly—up to 700 times a second—resulting in a lighthouse effect.
Evidence visual: Discovered in 1967, these cosmic clocks help measure time and test gravity.
Payoff visual: But uncertainty remains—what happens when pulsars die?
Cta visual: Want to dive deeper into space mysteries? Hit follow and explore together!
